H3000.derivation: from H1288 (בָרַךְ) and H3050 (יָהּ); blessed of Jah;

H3001.strongs_def: to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also (as failing) to dry up (as water) or wither (as herbage)
H3001.kjv_def: be ashamed, clean, be confounded, (make) dry (up), (do) shame(-fully), [idiom] utterly, wither (away).
H3001.usage: withered(22x), confounded(9x), ashamed(7x), dry(7x), clean(1x), shamed(1x), shamefully(1x), utterly(1x)

H3003.derivation: the same as H3002 (יָבֵשׁ) (also יָבֵישׁ; often with the addition of H1568 (גִּלְעָד), i.e. Jabesh of Gilad);
H3003.strongs_def: Jobesh, the name of an Israelite and of a place in Palestine
H3003.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H3005.derivation: from the same as H1314 (בֶּשֶׂם); fragrant;

H3008.strongs_def: Jigal, the name of three Israelites

H3011.strongs_def: Jogbehah, a place East of the Jordan

H3012.derivation: from H1431 (גָּדַל) and H3050 (יָהּ); magnified of Jah;

H3013.kjv_def: afflict, cause grief, grieve, sorrowful, vex.

H3014.derivation: a primitive root (probably rather the same as H3013 (יָגָה) through the common idea of dissatisfaction);

H3018.strongs_def: toil; hence, a work, produce, property (as the result of labor)

H3021.strongs_def: properly, to gasp; hence, to be exhausted, to tire, to toil

H3026.derivation: (Aramaic) from a word derived from an unused root (meaning to gather) and a derivation of a root corresponding to H7717 (שָׂהֵד); heap of the testimony;
H3026.strongs_def: Jegar-Sahadutha, a cairn East of the Jordan

H3027.derivation: a primitive word; in distinction from H3709 (כַּף), the closed one); used (as noun, adverb, etc.) in a great variety of applications, both literally and figuratively, both proximate and remote (as follows)
H3027.strongs_def: a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.),
H3027.kjv_def: ([phrase] be) able, [idiom] about, [phrase] armholes, at, axletree, because of, beside, border, [idiom] bounty, [phrase] broad, (broken-) handed, [idiom] by, charge, coast, [phrase] consecrate, [phrase] creditor, custody, debt, dominion, [idiom] enough, [phrase] fellowship, force, [idiom] from, hand(-staves, -y work), [idiom] he, himself, [idiom] in, labour, [phrase] large, ledge, (left-) handed, means, [idiom] mine, ministry, near, [idiom] of, [idiom] order, ordinance, [idiom] our, parts, pain, power, [idiom] presumptuously, service, side, sore, state, stay, draw with strength, stroke, [phrase] swear, terror, [idiom] thee, [idiom] by them, [idiom] themselves, [idiom] thine own, [idiom] thou, through, [idiom] throwing, [phrase] thumb, times, [idiom] to, [idiom] under, [idiom] us, [idiom] wait on, (way-) side, where, [phrase] wide, [idiom] with (him, me, you), work, [phrase] yield, [idiom] yourselves.
H3027.usage: by(44x), him(14x), power(12x), them(11x), places(8x), tenons(6x), thee(6x), coast(6x), side(5x)

H3028.strongs_def: {a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.)}

H3031.derivation: from the same as H1706 (דְּבַשׁ); perhaps honeyed;

H3032.strongs_def: properly, to handle, i.e. to throw, e.g. lots

H3033.strongs_def: properly, affection; concretely, a darling object

H3034.derivation: a primitive root; used only as denominative from H3027 (יָד); literally, to use (i.e. hold out) the hand;
H3034.strongs_def: physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
H3034.usage: praise(53x), confess(16x), thank(5x), thanksgiving(2x), cast(1x), shoot(1x), thankful(1x)

H3037.strongs_def: Jaddua, the name of two Israelites

H3038.derivation: or יְדֻתוּן; or יְדִיתוּן; probably from H3034 (יָדָה); laudatory;

H3041.derivation: from H3039 (יְדִיד) and H3050 (יָהּ); beloved of Jah;

H3042.derivation: from H3034 (יָדָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); praised of Jah;
H3042.strongs_def: Jedajah, the name of two Israelites

H3043.derivation: from H3045 (יָדַע) and H410 (אֵל); knowing God;
H3043.strongs_def: Jediael, the name of three Israelites

H3045.strongs_def: to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, instruction, designation, punishment, etc.)
H3045.kjv_def: acknowledge, acquaintance(-ted with), advise, answer, appoint, assuredly, be aware, (un-) awares, can(-not), certainly, comprehend, consider, [idiom] could they, cunning, declare, be diligent, (can, cause to) discern, discover, endued with, familiar friend, famous, feel, can have, be (ig-) norant, instruct, kinsfolk, kinsman, (cause to let, make) know, (come to give, have, take) knowledge, have (knowledge), (be, make, make to be, make self) known, [phrase] be learned, [phrase] lie by man, mark, perceive, privy to, [idiom] prognosticator, regard, have respect, skilful, shew, can (man of) skill, be sure, of a surety, teach, (can) tell, understand, have (understanding), [idiom] will be, wist, wit, wot.
H3045.usage: know(645x), known(105x), knowledge(19x), perceive(18x), shew(17x), tell(8x), wist(7x), understand(7x), certainly(7x), acknowledge(6x), acquaintance(6x), consider(6x), declare(6x), teach(5x)

H3046.strongs_def: {to know (properly, to ascertain by seeing); used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially (including observation, care, recognition; and causatively, instruction, designation, punishment, etc.)}

H3048.derivation: from H3045 (יָדַע) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has known;
H3048.strongs_def: Jedajah, the name of two Israelites

H3049.strongs_def: properly, a knowing one; specifically, a conjurer; (by impl) a ghost

H3050.derivation: contraction for H3068 (יְהֹוָה), and meaning the same;
H3050.kjv_def: Jah, the Lord, most vehement. Compare names in '-iah,' '-jah.'
H3050.pos: proper noun with reference to deity

H3051.strongs_def: to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put; imperatively (reflexive) come
H3051.kjv_def: ascribe, bring, come on, give, go, set, take.

H3052.strongs_def: {to give (whether literal or figurative); generally, to put; imperatively (reflexive) come}
H3052.kjv_def: deliver, give, lay, [phrase] prolong, pay, yield.
H3052.usage: give(21x), delivered(1x), laid(1x), paid(1x), prolonged(1x), yielded(1x)

H3053.strongs_def: properly, what is given (by Providence), i.e. a lot

H3054.derivation: denominative from a form corresponding to H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד;

H3055.derivation: a briefer form of one corresponding to H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד;

H3056.derivation: perhaps from a form corresponding to H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד; Judaistic;

H3058.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H1931 (הוּא); Jehovah (is) He;
H3058.strongs_def: Jehu, the name of five Israelites

H3059.strongs_def: Jehoachaz, the name of three Israelites

H3060.strongs_def: Jehoash, the name of two Israelite kings

H3061.derivation: (Aramaic) contracted from a form corresponding to H3063 (יְהוּדָה);

H3062.derivation: (Aramaic) patrial from H3061 (יְהוּד)lemma יְהיּד yod, corrected to יְהוּד;

H3063.strongs_def: Jehudah (or Judah), the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory

H3064.strongs_def: a Jehudite (i.e. Judaite or Jew), or descendant of Jehudah (i.e. Judah)

H3066.pos: feminine proper adjective (used as an adverb)

H3068.strongs_def: Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
H3068.kjv_def: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).
H3068.pos: proper noun with reference to deity

H3069.derivation: a variation of H3068 (יְהֹוָה) (used after H136 (אֲדֹנָי), and pronounced by Jews as H430 (אֱלֹהִים), in order to prevent the repetition of the same sound, since they elsewhere pronounce H3068 (יְהֹוָה) as H136 (אֲדֹנָי))
H3069.pos: proper noun with reference to deity

H3070.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H7200 (רָאָה); Jehovah will see (to it);
H3070.strongs_def: Jehovah-Jireh, a symbolical name for Mount Moriah

H3071.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5251 (נֵס) with the prononimal suffix; Jehovah (is) my banner;
H3071.strongs_def: Jehovah-Nissi, a symbolical name of an altar in the Desert

H3072.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H6664 (צֶדֶק) with pronominal suffix; Jehovah (is) our right;
H3072.strongs_def: Jehovah-Tsidkenu, a symbolical epithet of the Messiah and of Jerusalem

H3073.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H7965 (שָׁלוֹם); Jehovah (is) peace;
H3073.strongs_def: Jehovah-Shalom, a symbolical name of an altar in Palestine

H3074.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H8033 (שָׁם) with directive enclitic; Jehovah (is) thither;
H3074.strongs_def: Jehovah-Shammah, a symbolic title of Jerusalem

H3075.strongs_def: Jehozabad, the name of three Israelites

H3076.strongs_def: Jehochanan, the name of eight Israelites

H3077.strongs_def: Jehojada, the name of three Israelites

H3078.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H3559 (כּוּן); Jehovah will establish;

H3079.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) abbreviated and H6965 (קוּם); Jehovah will raise;

H3080.derivation: from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H7378 (רִיב); Jehovah will contend;
H3080.strongs_def: Jehojarib, the name of two Israelites

H3082.strongs_def: Jehonadab, the name of an Israelite and of an Arab

H3083.strongs_def: Jehonathan, the name of four Israelites

H3084.strongs_def: Jehoseph (i.e. Joseph), a son of Jacob

H3086.derivation: or יְהוֹעַדָּן; from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5727 (עָדַן); Jehovahpleased;

H3088.strongs_def: Jehoram, the name of a Syrian and of three Israelites

H3091.derivation: or יְהוֹשֻׁעַ; from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H3467 (יָשַׁע); Jehovah-saved;
H3091.kjv_def: Jehoshua, Jehoshuah, Joshua. Compare H1954 (הוֹשֵׁעַ), H3442 (יֵשׁוּעַ).

H3092.strongs_def: Jehoshaphat, the name of six Israelites; also of a valley near Jerusalem
H3092.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H3093.derivation: probably from the same as H2022 (הַר);

H3094.derivation: from H1984 (הָלַל) and H410 (אֵל); praising God;
H3094.strongs_def: Jehallelel, the name of two Israelites

H3095.derivation: from H1986 (הָלַם) (in the sense of hardness);

H3096.derivation: or יַהְצָה; or (feminine) יַהְצָה; from an unused root meaning to stamp; perhaps threshing-floor;
H3096.strongs_def: Jahats or Jahtsah, a place East of the Jordan

H3097.strongs_def: Joab, the name of three Israelites

H3098.strongs_def: Joach, the name of four Israelites

H3099.strongs_def: Joachaz, the name of two Israelites
